_meta.asset_split: purely relational — membership/roles, relations, group_levers with member_effects, derived_from edges. Lever nodes and per-marker read-constants live in lever_dictionary.json.
_meta.binding_note: Every marker_canonical / operand / member key is a marker_canonical.json v0.2 id. Anything that could not bind is under _deferred with its exact blocking id — nothing dangles.

relation_kinds.ratio: Interpretive signal is the ratio of two members (T:E2, De Ritis).
relation_kinds.co_movement: Members expected to move together; divergence is the signal.
relation_kinds.discriminator: One member's value localises/attributes another's movement.
relation_kinds.feedback: One member drives/suppresses another via a physiological loop; phase-gated.
relation_kinds.context: A member reframes another as watch/expected without fixed arithmetic; often cross-axis.

relations.reads: T:E2 proportionality — E2 tracking substrate vs disproportionate aromatisation.
On stable dosing these track the substrate pool together; divergence is the signal.
Exogenous androgen closes the hypothalamic–pituitary loop → LH/FSH suppressed. Expected on_trt; off-TRT the same suppression is news.
SHBG sets the bound fraction — a high SHBG lowers free-T at a given total, so read free-T against SHBG, not total alone.
AST:ALT — ≈1 chronic muscle, >2 alcoholic injury, <1 common in steatosis.
Normal GGT → transaminase rise is not hepatobiliary (points to muscle/exercise).
ALP and GGT rise together in cholestasis; isolated ALP (normal GGT) points to bone, not liver.
Bilirubin↑ with normal GGT/ALP and normal haemolysis_index/LD = isolated unconjugated → constitutional (Gilbert's), not cholestatic or haemolytic.

group_levers.de_select_hint: Runtime: de-select when ggt is normal (ggt_hepatobiliary_discriminator fires).

relations.reads: All three are concentrations of the same red cell line and move together; divergence points at a measurement or index change rather than a change in red cell mass.

Albumin and total protein are plasma-based and rise with plasma contraction, so a red cell rise with them rising is haemoconcentration (a draw artefact), while a red cell rise with albumin/protein flat is a true erythron expansion; MCV is per-cell and plasma-independent, so it stays flat in the artefact case.

group_levers.de_select_hint: Runtime: de-select when albumin is flat (haemoconcentration_discriminator fires). MCV is omitted from member_effects deliberately - it is the plasma-independent control and does not move.

derived_from.note: eGFR is computed from creatinine (+age/sex). Movement is attributed to creatinine, not surfaced as independent news (contract I6).
Vermeulen-type calc. Also a free extraction-QA gate: re-derive and compare to the reported value at confirm time.
AG = (Na + K) − (Cl + HCO3). Re-derivation is a consistency check on the electrolyte extraction.

_deferred.note: Deferred entries fall into THREE classes, not one. Only the first is discharged by expanding marker_canonical.json. (1) vocabulary gap — the marker is on the report but has no canonical id yet; a vocab bump clears it; blocked_on names the exact missing id(s). (2) never ordered — the marker could be canonical but has never been ordered, so no data arrives until it is; only ordering the test clears it; see _blocked_on_order. (3) lab does not report it — structurally absent from this provider's output; neither a bump nor an order clears it, so the entry must be re-scoped or retired; see retired. Prior to v0.3 this note claimed all deferrals were class (1). That was true of one of four entries, and made the block read as a single queue awaiting a single event.

erythroid.carries: the cross-axis context relation below — the contract's showcase cross-axis instance is unbuildable until these are canonical.

trt_erythrocytosis_watch.reads: On TRT, watch Hct for erythrocytosis — a protocol-driven context, not a breach of the erythroid axis itself.

ck_muscle_discriminator.reads: High CK with high AST → muscle origin — complements the GGT discriminator.

retired.retired_because: SNP prints only 'Calcium (Corrected)'. Uncorrected calcium is structurally absent from the report, so the correction can never be recomputed — and never needs to be.
retired.re_scoped_as: primitive — calcium_corrected has been canonical since v0.2 and is read directly off the report.

retired.class: none of the three — mis-filed as derived
retired.retired_because: the lab prints 'Non HDLC' directly, so non_hdl is a primitive read. Deriving it would recompute a reported number.
retired.re_scoped_as: primitive (canonical as of v0.3), plus the 4b QA identity below.

qa_identities.note: All three are printed by the lab as of v0.3. A cross-check on extraction fidelity, NOT a derivation — if it fails the extraction is wrong, not the arithmetic.
